title: Lepidella tetrantha
taxonomy: Lepidella tetrantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Podostemaceae. This family, Podostemaceae, is a small group of aquatic plants that are commonly known as river weeds. Lepidella tetrantha is native to Australia, specifically in the northern and eastern regions. The plant is usually found growing in streams and rivers, often in shallow water. It is a part of the larger group known as angiosperms or flowering plants.

intro: Lepidella tetrantha, commonly known as the 'Four-flowered tree', is a medicinal plant species native to Australia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Lepidella tetrantha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
